title: hedyotis graminifolia subsp. arenaria
intro: Hedyotis graminifolia subsp. arenaria, also known as creeping St. John's wort, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits associated with creeping St. John's wort include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or decoction for medicinal purposes, and it has been used in herbal remedies to treat conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and minor skin irritations.
